pages.full_text:
at 11.30. Trinity Sunday. Flat the prayer-book & in Welsh. We stopped for a paper & Coke on the way back to the campsite.
	The weather is gorgeous; Blue skies & sun, but a slightly chilly wind. Tonie stayed in the loo during church. The boys built another dam before lunch.
		In the meadows are William & Jeffrey write John & Charles alternating. Thomas is sleeping in the pyramid tent, with Tonie's tied up. Peter is in his new hammock. The wardrobe is done by William & Charles, Thomas & John & Peter & Jeffrey.
		In the afternoon Simon, Eve, Thomas Tonie John(reluctantly) & Charles went for a walk in the hills, while Jeffrey, Peter & William made an even better dam, with a log. We had Peter's chocolate birthday cake! A Liverpool family from years ago are here again - Gawain & Oliver.
		We were going to have lamb chops in abby in the evening, but they were still frozen. The boys made a campfire & toasted marshmallows!
pages.notes: Some text slightly difficult to read due to handwriting flow and pressure variation. 'abby' on final line is unclear - could be 'abbey' or similar. Reading 'Tonie's' and 'Tonie' as likely spellings. 'Gawain' could also read 'Gwain'.

pages.full_text:
Saturday 26th May
	We left at 10 to Rhino. Old Oswee's road ladder style. Bog, old paths, lovely creaks of the sea. Tuwee Tonie chased a sheep, the second time, when Thomas dropped the load, but ran fast & caught him. The harness has been chasing three behind his back, so he's back to twist a collar. Tonie missed the second sheep, because he paused at the water. We paused just before the summit & had lunch - sunflower bread, peanuts, pork pies, crisps, prunes, bananas & water. Thomas would have popped sausages, rather.
		We walked back past campsite near the river in the valley bottom. The climb had been very step sometimes on all 4's going up & slithering down - 7 miles.
		Simon & Thomas costed lamb chops for supper on the open fire. Peter costed veg.

Tuesday 27th May
	Breakfast at 8.30. Then we set off for Harlech & looked at the castle. Peter bought a kite, John a pack of cards & Charles a bit of weapons. We drove on to Porthmadog to see Eric Bett's slate factory - purple, grey, green slate. We had fish & chips & wandered
pages.notes: Some words are challenging: 'Oswee's' or 'Oswees' unclear - possibly 'Osexes' or similar place name. 'Rhino' may be 'Rhian' or variant. 'Tuwee' unclear - possibly 'Twice'. 'creaks' likely 'creeks'. 'step' likely 'steep'. 'costed' likely 'cooked'. 'Eric Bett's' - surname unclear, could be 'Bott' or 'Betts'. Underlines appear to mark place names.
